name: Vegetable Stock
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the margarine in an 8-quart pot over medium heat.
2. Add the potatoes, onion, carrots, parsnips, and parsley to the pot. Brown well, stirring frequently.
3. Add the water, peppercorns, bay leaves, and dill seed.
4. Bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 1.5 hours, covered.
5. Remove and discard the bay leaves. Allow the stock to cool completely.
6. Strain the stock through a fine-mesh sieve into another pot or container.
7. Salt to taste. Discard any remaining vegetables.
8. Refrigerate the stock for several hours to allow it to set up. Once set, remove the congealed fat from the surface.
Makes 3.5 Quarts of Stock
NOTE: This is a rich brown stock that can be used in place of beef stock in some recipes. Each 1 cup serving contains:
